summary: The test suite covers core requirements (manifest parsing, license compliance logic, report generation, workflow integration) but has significant gaps. Unit tests use mocking appropriately and test happy paths plus basic errors (file not found, invalid JSON). However, coverage is incomplete: (1) no testing of alternative manifest formats (requirements.txt, etc.) mentioned in task requirements; (2) workflow integration has only 2 test cases when many scenarios should be tested (all-denied, all-unknown, empty manifest, error cases); (3) critical behavior untested—no validation that script exits with code 2 when denied licenses found, no JSON output structure validation beyond regex matching, no special character/encoding tests. Edge case rigor is weak: missing boundary conditions, test cases for licenses on both allow/deny lists (except one unit test), and various error scenarios. Test design is reasonable (clear Describe blocks, BeforeAll/BeforeEach usage, mocking) but the workflow test BeforeAll is complex with manual setup, and test data is minimally documented. This suite would catch regressions in core parsing/compliance logic and workflow execution, but would miss exit code behavior changes, format/output changes, and many edge case failures.
